title: P1-6 doctor --probe JSON readiness snapshot
description: Add fabric doctor --probe that emits a machine-readable JSON readiness snapshot without running the full --fix mutation pipeline.
action: Add boolean flag probe to packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts DoctorArgs + citty args; when --probe (optionally with --json default true for probe), call a pure readiness builder that reuses assessFirstHit / storeDoctorChecks signals and prints JSON { ok, first_hit: FirstHitReport-like, stores: [...], hooks: {...}, write_target, issues_summary } without invoking runDoctorFix or runDoctorFixKnowledge.
scope: packages/cli/src/commands + packages/cli/src/store + packages/server doctor types if needed

criteria: packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts DoctorArgs includes `probe?: boolean` and citty args.probe type boolean
When probe path runs, code path does not call runDoctorFix or runDoctorFixKnowledge (rg -n 'probe' doctor.ts shows early branch)
Probe JSON includes at least keys: `ok` (boolean) and `first_hit` (object with `code` field matching FirstHitCode union) OR equivalent documented schema
Unit/CLI test asserts --probe output is valid JSON and ok is boolean
convergence.verification: pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -- doctor; rg -n 'probe' packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts packages/cli/__tests__
convergence.definition_of_done: CI/scripts can call fabric doctor --probe --json for readiness without mutation side effects.

files.change: Add probe flag (EXPOSED_FLAGS or intentional advanced). On probe: build readiness JSON from assessFirstHit + lightweight store diagnostics; writeStdout JSON; skip fix pipeline; exit non-zero when first_hit not ok / readiness failed (mirror first-hit exit policy or document probe-specific exit)
If needed, export a compact readiness DTO builder used by both first-hit and doctor --probe (avoid duplicating remediation logic)
Add exported type for probe snapshot if server package is the shared type home; else keep type local to CLI
Describe --probe as machine JSON readiness without --fix
Invoke doctor command/handler with probe:true; parse JSON; assert shape; ensure no fix mutations when probe set

implementation: Reuse assessFirstHit({ projectRoot }) from first-hit.ts as the core readiness oracle
Compose probe report: { schema_version: 1, ok: report.ok, first_hit: report, generated_at: ISO }
Wire --probe to force JSON output (or require --json; prefer probe implies json for machine use)
Do not run migrateRootConfig mutations beyond read-only resolution if avoidable
Add tests using existing doctor test harness temp project fixtures

success_metrics: no --fix side effects on probe path

reference.pattern: fabric first-hit --json readiness + doctor --json report branch
reference.examples: first-hit already proves install→bind→surface readiness; doctor --probe packages it for health scripts without full doctor report cost if possible

rationale.chosen_approach: Compose existing first-hit oracle under doctor --probe rather than inventing a third readiness system.
decision_factors: doctor keeps health surface; audit stays separate
rationale.tradeoffs: Probe may duplicate some first-hit CLI surface — acceptable if doctor is the ops entrypoint

risks: Probe accidentally runs --fix path — gate with early return before fix
Exit code policy conflict with doctor --json warn exits — document probe uses first-hit exit codes
